heading: Omitted
label: 485 to 486j Omitted
notes: CodificationSections 485 to 486, relating to Territory of Alaska, were omitted in view of admission of Alaska into the Union.Section 485, acts , ; , , , authorized Administrator of Civil Aeronautics to construct, protect, operate, improve, and maintain within Territory of Alaska a public airport at or near Anchorage and a public airport at or near Fairbanks.May 28, 1948, ch. 354, § 162 Stat. 277Pub. L. 85–726, title XIV, § 1402(d)(1)72 Stat. 807Aug. 23, 1958Section 485a, , , authorized Administrator of Civil Aeronautics to acquire by purchase, lease, condemnation or otherwise such lands and appurtenances necessary for construction, protection, maintenance, improvement, and operation of said airports.act May 28, 1948, ch. 354, § 262 Stat. 278Section 485b, , , authorized Administrator to acquire rights-of-way or easements for roads, trails, pipe lines, power lines and other similar facilities necessary for operation of airports, and to construct any public highways and bridge to whatever airport locations may be selected.act May 28, 1948, ch. 354, § 362 Stat. 278Section 485c, acts , ; , , , set out powers and duties of Administrator.May 28, 1948, ch. 354, § 462 Stat. 278Pub. L. 85–726, title XIV, § 1402(d)(2)72 Stat. 807Aug. 23, 1958Section 485d, acts , ; , ; , , ; , , , empowered Administrator of Federal Aviation Agency to lease space or property within or upon airports.May 28, 1948, ch. 354, § 562 Stat. 278Oct. 10, 1951, ch. 45765 Stat. 371Pub. L. 85–50372 Stat. 321Pub. L. 85–726, title XIV, § 1402(d)(3)72 Stat. 807July 3, 1958Aug. 23, 1958Section 485e, , , authorized Administrator to contract with any person for performance of services at or upon airports.act May 28, 1948, ch. 354, § 662 Stat. 278Section 485f, acts , ; , , authorized transfer of lands, building, property or equipment by other agencies of Federal Government to Administrator.May 28, 1948, ch. 354, § 762 Stat. 278Oct. 31, 1951, ch. 654, § 2(25)65 Stat. 707Section 485g, , , provided for penalties for violations of any rule, regulation or order issued by Administrator.act May 28, 1948, ch. 354, § 862 Stat. 278Section 485h, , , prescribed definitions used in sections 485 to 485h of this title, should be definitions assigned by the Civil Aeronautics Act of 1938, as amended.act May 28, 1948, ch. 354, § 962 Stat. 279Section 486, , , declared Congressional purpose of sections 486 to 486j of this title was to foster settlement and increase permanent residents of Alaska.act Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 263 Stat. 627Section 486a, , , authorized Secretary of the Interior to accept applications for public works.act Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 363 Stat. 627Section 486b, , , authorized Secretary to include works from other Federal agencies in the public works program.act Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 463 Stat. 627Section 486c, acts , ; , , ; , , , empowered Secretary to enter into agreement with public work applicant.Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 563 Stat. 628Pub. L. 85–233, § 171 Stat. 515Pub. L. 88–22977 Stat. 471Aug. 30, 1957Dec. 23, 1963Section 486d, , , set out authority and powers of applicants for public work.act Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 663 Stat. 628Section 486e, , , provided for cooperation between other Federal agencies and Secretary, and the transfer of jurisdiction from other Federal agencies to Secretary.act Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 763 Stat. 629Section 486f, , , authorized Secretary to provide public works through the award of contracts.act Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 863 Stat. 629Section 486g, , , directed that all moneys received by Secretary should be covered into Treasury as miscellaneous receipts.act Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 963 Stat. 629Section 486h, , , authorized Secretary to utilize and act through other Federal agencies.act Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 1063 Stat. 629Section 486i, , , provided for appropriations to carry out purposes of sections 486 to 486j.act Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 1163 Stat. 629Section 486j, acts , ; , , directed that authority of Secretary under sections 486 to 486j of this title shall terminate on .Aug. 24, 1949, ch. 504, § 1263 Stat. 629July 15, 1954, ch. 51068 Stat. 483June 30, 1959
